purpose: Explain why V2-H hardened ASM policy was not runtime-confirmed and what V2-I raw recovery changed.

rows.jarPolicy: skip signed/MR/sealed jars and BootstrapMethods classes
skip signed/MR/sealed jars; preserve BootstrapMethods while reducing LVT/LVTT

interpretation: V2-F hardening removed jar-level risk by skipping signed, multi-release, and sealed jars.
V2-F/H also skipped 6,029 BootstrapMethods-bearing classes under the ASM engine.
V2-I keeps the V2-F jar-level skips but uses a raw byte-preserving engine for BootstrapMethods-bearing classes.
V2-I recovered the BootstrapMethods class surface, but did not recreate the old V2-E artifact byte delta because raw rewriting preserves class bytes more literally than ASM round-tripping.
Runtime behavior, not artifact bytes alone, determined promotion: V2-I passed V2-C confirmation while V2-H failed its screen.
